rxn_class_summary: Iodide-mediated electrocatalytic aziridination of simple aromatic alkenes (styrenes) with NH3 to give unprotected N-H aziridines and H2
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of I- to I2 (I+ equivalent), which reacts with NH3 to form the electrophilic nitrogen transfer reagent NH2I; H+/NH4+ is reduced at the Ni cathode to H2
electron_flow: At the carbon anode 2 I- are oxidized to I2, which reacts with NH3 to generate NH2I (plus NH4+ and I-); NH2I aminohalogenates styrene to a vicinal iodoamine that undergoes base-assisted anti cyclization to the N-H aziridine, releasing I-, while NH4+/H2O is reduced at the Ni cathode to H2, closing the EC' cycle.
rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of I- to I+/I2 coupled to its chemical reaction with NH3 (EC' step generating NH2I); supported by quasilinear CV response only when both iodide and ammonia are present

probe_reagents.name: I2 (1 equiv) without current
probe_reagents.observation: 12% aziridine, indicating I2 is (one of) the active species generated by anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: Na2S2O3 quench
probe_reagents.observation: Dark red/brown I2 color disappears, regenerating I-, confirming presence of I2/I+ species

probe_reagents.name: 15NH4OH labeling
probe_reagents.observation: 1H/15N HMBC shows aziridine 15N at ~16 ppm; new 15N peaks at 103 and 256 ppm assigned to NH2I and NHI2

probe_reagents.name: UV-Vis spectrophotometry on reaction without styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Spectrum matches triiodide, confirming I2 generation at the anode

notes: An alternative cathodic half-reaction is direct reduction of water to H2 + OH- (equivalent to NH4+ reduction). The authors note steric and electronic substrate effects: α-substituted styrenes give very high yields (stabilization of partial positive charge at α-carbon during NH2I attack), while β-stilbenes give only traces due to sterics; this supports an electrophilic aminohalogenation pathway via NH2I rather than a free aminyl radical.
